target.title: Document public-corpus skill publishing boundary
target.body:
## Summary

This PR codifies the public-corpus skill provenance boundary for maintainers and contributors.

- routes direct skill additions in `AGENTS.md` to the dev-seeding boundary docs
- documents owner-scoped publishing as the canonical path for community/publisher skills
- records public-corpus refresh expectations: source snapshot, redaction policy, row counts, and validation output
- clarifies fixture-local dummy seeded owners as corpus scaffolding separate from publisher provenance

## Trust / security impact

The patch tightens the provenance boundary around skill publication. Community and publisher skill contributions are represented by authenticated owner-scoped publishing, while public-corpus refreshes are governed as maintainer-prepared fixture snapshots with explicit source and validation evidence.

The manifest wording keeps dummy seeded owners in the fixture-scaffolding role rather than treating them as publisher identity.

## Verification

Current head: `5ce7ba5503d0b9035f64c141388cf856e00711f6`

```console
$ bun run validate:public-corpus
$ bun scripts/public-corpus/validate-public-corpus.ts
{
  "ok": true,
  "fixture": "fixtures/public-corpus/corpus.jsonl",
  "rowCount": 1250,
  "skillCount": 1000,
  "pluginCount": 250
}
```

```console
$ bun test scripts/public-corpus/validate.test.ts scripts/public-corpus/seed-public-corpus.test.ts
bun test v1.3.14 (0d9b296a)

scripts/public-corpus/seed-public-corpus.test.ts:
(pass) public corpus seed runner > recognizes retryable Convex write conflicts [0.04ms]
(pass) public corpus seed runner > retries retryable batch conflicts before succeeding [0.20ms]
(pass) public corpus seed runner > does not retry non-conflict failures [0.04ms]

scripts/public-corpus/validate.test.ts:
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > accepts clean skill and plugin rows [0.44ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > rejects owner identity fields and raw Convex ids [0.13ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > rejects duplicate slugs within each artifact kind [1.17ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > rejects empty content and secret-like text [0.15ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > rejects raw local absolute paths but allows redacted path placeholders [0.06ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > parses newline-delimited corpus rows [0.22ms]
(pass) public corpus fixture validation > documents the committed fixture path [0.01ms]

 10 pass
 0 fail
 22 expect() calls
Ran 10 tests across 2 files. [86.00ms]
```

```console
$ bun run ci:static
$ bun run check:peers && bun audit --ignore GHSA-rmmr-r34h-pfm5 && bun run format:check && bun run lint && bun run deadcode:ci
$ bun scripts/check-peer-deps.ts
peer ok: @auth/core 0.37.4 satisfies @convex-dev/auth (^0.37.0)
bun audit v1.3.14 (0d9b296a)
No vulnerabilities found
$ oxfmt --check
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 912ms on 845 files using 10 threads.
$ bun run lint:oxlint
$ oxlint --type-aware --tsconfig ./tsconfig.oxlint.json ./src ./convex ./packages/clawhub/src ./packages/clawhub-mod/src ./packages/schema/src
$ bun run deadcode:knip
$ bun run deadcode:files && bun run deadcode:dependencies && bun run deadcode:exports
$ bunx knip@6.8.0 --config knip.config.ts --production --no-progress --reporter compact --files --no-config-hints
$ bunx knip@6.8.0 --config knip.config.ts --production --no-progress --reporter compact --dependencies --no-config-hints
$ KNIP_INCLUDE_TESTS=1 bunx knip@6.8.0 --config knip.config.ts --no-progress --reporter compact --exports --no-config-hints
```
